text:
You are an expert AI researcher, medical imaging engineer, and full-stack ML architect.

I want to build a commercial-grade application that takes an image of a human eye, analyzes it using computer vision and deep learning, and outputs:

Whether the eye is normal or infected

If infected, which specific eye condition (example: conjunctivitis, stye, cataract, blepharitis, corneal ulcer, dry eye, red eye, glaucoma indicators, etc.)

A confidence score for the prediction

A clear explanation of visual features detected (redness, discharge, cloudiness, swelling, pupil abnormality)

Requirements:

Use CNN / Vision Transformer / Hybrid models

Suggest publicly available medical eye datasets (no paid datasets)

Include data preprocessing steps (cropping eye region, normalization, augmentation)

Include model training pipeline

Include evaluation metrics (accuracy, recall, precision, F1, confusion matrix)

Include deployment architecture

Include ethical & medical disclaimer handling

Application Architecture:

Frontend: Image upload (mobile + web)

Backend: Python (FastAPI / Flask)

ML Framework: TensorFlow or PyTorch

Inference: Real-time (<2 seconds)

Scalability: API-based design

Output Format:

System architecture diagram (textual)

Dataset recommendations with links

Model choice + reasoning

Step-by-step training pipeline

Inference logic

Sample Python code (training + inference)

UI/UX flow for user

Medical safety disclaimer text

Build this assuming the app is educational / screening-only, not a replacement for a doctor.

Think step-by-step and design it as a real-world deployable AI product, not a demo.
